title: sphaeranthus peduncularis subsp. rogersii
intro: Sphaeranthus peduncularis subsp. rogersii is a type of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, commonly known as a species of the button bush. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating various infections, due to its ability to modulate the immune system. In traditional medicine, Sphaeranthus peduncularis subsp. rogersii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used as a topical cream or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
